bids.reasoning: At the start of the negotiation (time_fraction = 0.0), my strategy is to propose an offer that maximizes my own utility. Given this, I will select the highest utility values for each issue based on my preference context. Since there have been no offers exchanged yet and no messages from the opponent, I have no information on their preferences or constraints. Therefore, my initial offer will involve my top preference values across all issues.

At this early stage of the negotiation (t=0.02), there's ample time to aim for a high-utility offer. The opponent's first offer has very low utility for me, as it includes many low-preference values. I will counter with a bid close to my initial offer to see if there's any movement from the opponent, holding on to my top preferences for most issues, especially for the critical issueE.

In this early stage of negotiation, my goal is to maintain a strong position by sticking close to my preferences. I will slightly adjust by conceding minimally on a low-priority issue to show a willingness to negotiate.

At time fraction 0.06, it's early in the negotiation process, allowing me to remain firm on higher utility values. The opponent has repeated a low utility offer for me, showing no sign of compromise yet. I'll continue maximizing my utility by maintaining high-value preferences in most issues but will consider starting to explore minor concessions in less critical issues if necessary.
